title: THE TUB Harness + Mode Contract
thesis_claim: THE TUB operationalizes machine agency as bounded, audible behavior through locked protocol contracts and mode-specific sonic affordances.
cybernetic_loop: Live input features drive model outputs, mode contract clamps unsafe or invalid behavior, and audible outcomes feed back into performer labeling and mode steering.
material_system: Swift harness app, mode manifests, model server, replay bundles, and deterministic safety rails.
agency_model: Bounded autonomy with strict parameter envelopes and explicit contract fingerprinting.

evidence_refs.label: Harness Readme
evidence_refs.excerpt: At startup, the app validates mode-default picks against manifests and falls back deterministically when IDs are missing.

evidence_refs.label: Mode Contract
evidence_refs.excerpt: Protocol v2 contract fingerprint is locked and mismatches are surfaced as release blockers.

evidence_refs.label: Research Context
evidence_refs.excerpt: Technological systems are treated as performers with memory, temperament, and refusal.

risk_notes: Protocol drift between harness and model can silently invalidate artistic intent if lock checks are ignored.
Without audible mode verification, parameter correctness may hide perceptual regressions.
